records.ety: [L. transcriptum, neut. of transcriptus, p. p. of transcribere. See Transcribe.]

defs: That which has been transcribed; a writing or composition consisting of the same words as the original; a written copy.
quotes: The decalogue of Moses was but a transcript.

defs: A copy of any kind; an imitation.
A written version of what was said orally; as, a transcript of a trial.
quotes: The Grecian learning was but a transcript of the Chaldean and Egyptian.
